retry_reason: If the block group is already in a list, retrying immediately will still fail; the condition is stable until the block group is removed, so retry is pointless.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is list management (adding block group to a list), not refcount manipulation. The refcount increment via btrfs_get_block_group is a necessary step to protect the block group while on the list, but the function's core role is linking, not get/put/set. It returns a bool indicating whether the addition occurred, not a reference-counted object.
judge.retry_reason: If the block group is already in a list, retrying immediately will still fail; the condition is stable until the block group is removed, so retry is pointless.

final.retry_reason: If the block group is already in a list, retrying immediately will still fail; the condition is stable until the block group is removed, so retry is pointless.
